title: Major Global Brands Join Sustainability LIVE at London Climate Action Week 2026
author: Not Specified
description: Sustainability LIVE: The Leadership Summit announced the addition of 13 Chief Sustainability Officers and sustainability leaders from major global organizations ahead of London Climate Action Week 2026. The expanded speaker line-up includes executives from PepsiCo, Philip Morris International, RELX, ADM, and VINCI Group, among others. The summit will focus on practical insights regarding decarbonisation, sustainable supply chains, and climate action strategies.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Featured as a key speaker at a major sustainability leadership summit, demonstrating commitment to corporate sustainability transformation and climate action initiatives.
Represented by Chief Sustainability Officer at a prominent sustainability summit, indicating active engagement in sustainability leadership and corporate environmental initiatives.
Included among global organizations contributing sustainability expertise to the summit, signaling commitment to environmental and governance priorities.
Chief Sustainability Officer speaking on sustainable supply chains and decarbonisation, demonstrating proactive leadership in building resilient and transparent value chains.

title: SupplySide Connect New Jersey 2026 Advances Health and Wellness Through Industry Connections and Curated Education
author: Informa Markets
description: SupplySide Connect New Jersey 2026 concluded with 260+ exhibitors and major industry players attending to discuss health and wellness innovations, regulatory compliance, and supply chain solutions. The event highlighted growing consumer demand for transparency and personalized health products, with key topics including women's and men's health, longevity, gut health, and global expansion opportunities. SupplySide Connect will expand to new locations in Utah and Madison in 2027, with New Jersey returning in 2028.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Company attended the event as a notable participant, indicating engagement with industry trends and networking, but no specific business developments or announcements were mentioned.
Listed as an attendee at the event, showing participation in industry connections and product discovery, but no specific initiatives or outcomes were detailed.
Attended the event to discover new products and connections, indicating interest in health and nutrition sector, but no specific business impact or announcements were mentioned.
Participated as a notable attendee, demonstrating engagement with supplement and food industry innovations, but no specific developments or outcomes were disclosed.

title: Citric Acid Market Size to Surpass USD 5.10 Billion by 2035, Driven by Rising Demand Across Food, Pharma, and Industrial Applications | Research by SNS Insider
author: Sns Insider
description: The global citric acid market is projected to grow from USD 3.48 billion in 2025 to USD 5.10 billion by 2035, at a CAGR of 3.90%. Growth is driven by increasing demand in food & beverage, pharmaceutical, and cleaning applications, with Asia-Pacific dominating at 52.35% market share. Anhydrous citric acid and food-grade products lead by segment, while fermentation remains the primary production method.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: ADM launched a new online storefront (ADMbuydirect.com) in May 2025 to enhance accessibility and streamline procurement of citric acid products, demonstrating active market engagement and innovation in customer service.
Identified as a key market player but no specific recent developments or strategic initiatives mentioned in the article.

title: The Top 2 Consumer Staples Stocks to Buy Right Now
author: Patrick Sanders
description: Two consumer staples stocks are outperforming the S&P 500 with gains exceeding 10% this year. Archer-Daniels-Midland (ADM) has achieved 53 consecutive years of dividend increases and projects significantly improved 2026 earnings. Hershey (HSY) is reorganizing its brand portfolio and forecasting 30-35% earnings growth for 2026, with both stocks offering dividend yields well above the S&P 500's 1.15%.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Company projects 2026 earnings of $3.60-$4.25 versus 2025 EPS of $2.23, representing significant growth. Achieved 53 consecutive years of dividend increases (Dividend King status), stock up 24% year-to-date, and management expects $500-750 million in cost savings over three to five years.

Company forecasts 2026 sales growth of 4-5% and adjusted earnings of $8.20-$8.52, representing 30-35% increase from prior year. Stock up nearly 15% year-to-date. Strategic portfolio reorganization to centralize marketing and capitalize on brand power, though recent acquisitions caused temporary earnings decline.

title: Feed Additives Market Set to Reach $67.90 Billion by 2031 Driven by Rising Demand for Animal Nutrition
author: Marketsandmarkets
description: The global feed additives market is estimated at USD 50.72 billion in 2026 and projected to reach USD 67.90 billion by 2031, growing at a CAGR of 6.0%. Rising demand for animal-based protein, expanding commercial livestock production, and increasing adoption of advanced nutritional solutions are driving market growth. Asia Pacific is expected to register the fastest growth, while amino acids and dry feed additives are projected to lead their respective segments.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Identified as a key company shaping the feed additives market; well-positioned to capitalize on rising global demand for animal nutrition solutions and commercial farming expansion.
Key market participant with focus on feed additives innovation; expected to benefit from sustained market growth driven by rising protein demand and commercial farming practices.
Major global player in feed additives market; positioned to gain from expanding livestock sectors across Asia Pacific and increasing demand for scientifically formulated feed solutions.
Key supplier of amino acids, a segment expected to hold significant market share; benefits from growing reliance on amino acids in poultry, swine, and aquaculture sectors.
